_doc: Hashtag pool and tone notes for the caption-library skill. Edit freely — skills read this at runtime.

principles: Short by default — one CTA max
Adult: technical when visual is technical; reflective when portrait/lifestyle
Kids: warm, parent-facing, never sales-y, parent-trust-building
No emoji walls; one tasteful emoji at most
Sentence case — no ALL CAPS in captions
End with a quiet sign-off line, not a hard CTA

adult_technical.audience: adult BJJ practitioners, scrolling Tuesday evening
tone_notes: Name the technique or position when known (from brief.md).
Lead with the detail that separates it — grip, angle, timing.
Credit the instructor. Cobrinha or Dani is the usual answer.
Sign off with the brand line OR a quiet question that invites engagement (no hashtags-as-questions).
examples:
DLR sweep. The grip break is the difference.

Prof. Cobrinha — Tuesday class.

It's not the entry. It's the angle after the entry.

With Prof. Dani.

tone_notes: Speak to the work behind the moment, the team, the standard.
No hard sell. No 'book your trial today.' That's a different post.
If the photo has multiple people, lean into community language.
examples: Same room. Same mats. Different version of you every week.

kids_confidence.audience: parents of kids 4-12, scrolling Thursday evening
tone_notes: Lead with the kid's experience — confidence, friendship, focus, small wins.
Address the parent's worry quietly — safety, structure, character.
Soft CTA at most ('DM us to book a free trial' is fine; avoid 'limited time' or urgency hacks).
examples:
Today she earned her first stripe. Last month she was nervous to step on the mat.

Kids BJJ at Alliance Cobrinha LA — ages 4-12.

The mat teaches what classrooms can't: how to fall, get up, and try again.

DM us for a free trial.

kids_summer.audience: parents looking for summer programs, scrolling Saturday evening
tone_notes: Lean into the seasonal angle when in season — Beat the Heat, Active Defense, Best Summer Skill.
Energy is welcome here. Joy and action language.
Name the program (summer camp, summer pass) and the age range.
Soft CTA — 'spots filling up' is fine; avoid fake urgency.
examples:
Climate-controlled. Coach-led. The best place for kids when it's 100° outside.

Summer Camp — ages 5-12. DM to enroll.

The skill they'll use forever. The summer they'll never forget.

Kids BJJ Summer Pass — link in bio.

_mix_recipe._doc: How to assemble a hashtag set per post. Total 8-15 hashtags.
_mix_recipe.by_pillar: Pick 4-6 from the pillar's array; rotate which ones based on (week_number % len) so consecutive weeks don't reuse the exact same set
_mix_recipe.location: Pick 2 from location array; rotate similarly
_mix_recipe.rotating: Pick 1-2 from the rotating array; rotate
